name: Inca Festival Bread
recipeIngredient: Remainder of Cheddar Cheese, cut into 1/4 inch cubes
1/2 cup Coarsely Chopped Hot Chilies (fresh or canned)
4 ounces Whole Sweet Red Pimentos, halved
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small saucepan, melt 6 tablespoons butter over low heat without browning.
2. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
3. Place the remaining 2 tablespoons butter in an 8-inch diameter baking casserole dish and heat in the oven for no more than 4 to 5 minutes to avoid burning, rotating and tilting the casserole to coat the inside. Set aside.
4. In a large mixing bowl, beat together milk and eggs. Blend smoothly with 1 cup white cornmeal, melted butter, corn, grated cheddar cheese, chopped chilies, halved pimentos, baking soda, and salt. Mix thoroughly until it has the consistency of southern cornbread, moderately runny but not excessively liquid. If it seems too runny, add 1 or 2 tablespoons more white cornmeal.
5. Give the butter in the casserole a final swizzle, then pour in the batter, smoothing it level. Sprinkle grated cheddar cheese over the top and decorate with pimento strips.
6. Do not cover. Place the casserole exactly in the center of the oven.
7. Bake until a knife inserted into the center comes out clean and dry, usually 40 to 50 minutes. Serve immediately.
8. Cut into fairly thin, pie-shaped wedges. You can let the bread cool to room temperature before serving. It stores well tightly wrapped in the refrigerator but is best not served cold. One loaf makes about 8 servings.
